pages.raw_text:
take notice that the petitioner, the
Spokane International Railway Com


pany. has filed in the superior court


OF the state of Washington, for Spo-


Kane county. a petition directed to the


said court, setting forth that the Spo-


Kane International Railway Company


Is corporation organized under the


laws of the state Of Washington, en-


gagged in the construction and opera


ton of line of railway beginning


In the city of Spokane, thence easter


ly to point near the town of Rath-


drum, In the county of Kootenai,


state of Idaho, and thence in a north


easterly direction up the valley of


the Mooyie river, to the international


boundary line between the United
States and Canada; that ii Is essential
for the operation of it's lines Of rail-
way, and the accommodation of the
public using the same, that it acquire
those lands situated in Spokane coun-
ty, Washington, and described as lots
one CD and two ca in block four
ca, and lots three CD and four ca
in block one CD of River Front Ad-
dition to Spokane Falls (now Spo-
kane), Washington, for use for it's
railway, and the necessary sidetracks,
depot grounds and terminal yards;
that the defendant, B. T. Lacy. ap-
pears to be the sole owner in fee of
said realty, and the defendants,
- Lacy, his wife, and Thomas
w. Savage, have or claim to have
some interest in or lien upon said
premises; and that said petition prays
| that the court empanel a jury to
ascertain and determine the compen-
sation to be made in money to the
owner of said realty, and to all others


interested therein for the taking of


said realty by petitioner for the pur-
poses aforesaid;


| And you, and each of you, are here
by notified that on the 12th day of
July, 1905, petitioner will apply to the
superior court of the state of Wash
ington. for Spokane county, and to


the Honorable William A. Huneke,


judge thereof, at his courtroom in the


city Of Spokane, at the hour of $50
o'clock a. M, or as soon thereafter as


counsel for petitioner can be heard,


for a hearing upon said petition, and


an adjudication by the court that the


taking of said realty is for public


use, and is necessary to the business


of petitioner, and for the fixing of n


time and place at which a jury may


be called to determine the compensa-
ton to be made therefor.


Dated this 18th day of June, 1905,
ALLEN & ALLEN,
Attorneys for Petitioners.
p. o. address: 61S The Rookery,
Spokane, Wash.
